files.FILE-1: Create calculator.py in the current directory.
files.FILE-2: Create test_calculator.py in the current directory.
files.FILE-3: Do not read or write outside the current directory.

api.API-1: calculator.py exposes a callable function for binary arithmetic.
api.API-2: The binary arithmetic API supports +, -, *, and /.
api.API-3: Unsupported binary operators raise an error.
api.API-4: Division by zero raises an error.
api.API-5: calculator.py has a CLI entrypoint guarded by if __name__ == "__main__".

behavior.BEH-1: Addition returns the numeric sum of two operands.
behavior.BEH-2: Subtraction returns the numeric difference of two operands.
behavior.BEH-3: Multiplication returns the numeric product of two operands.
behavior.BEH-4: Division returns the numeric quotient of two operands.
behavior.BEH-5: CLI input handles the four binary operators and reports invalid input as an error.
behavior.BEH-6: Expression precedence, multi-operator expression parsing, symbolic algebra, unary functions, and stateful calculator memory are not public obligations in this case.

generated_test_contract.TEST-1: Generated tests assert only listed FILE/API/BEH/VERIFY requirements.
generated_test_contract.TEST-2: Generated tests do not introduce expression grammar, precedence, unary function, memory, or formatting obligations not listed in this contract.
generated_test_contract.TEST-3: Assertions reference requirement ids where practical.
generated_test_contract.TEST-4: Out-of-contract generated test obligations are GeneratedTestOutOfScope or TestViolatesContract, not source bugs.

repair_ownership.HARNESS-1: Verification failures pass Contract Reconciliation before repair dispatch.
repair_ownership.HARNESS-2: FILE/API/BEH/VERIFY failures are SourceViolatesContract unless the generated test contradicts this contract.
repair_ownership.HARNESS-3: TEST failures are TestViolatesContract or GeneratedTestOutOfScope and do not dispatch source repair.
repair_ownership.HARNESS-4: Failures without a scenario contract requirement id are ContractInsufficient and fail closed.
